channel.title: Home on Cottage Painter
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Cottage Painter

item.title: About
item.description:
My name is Currie Carmichael, I’m a professional painter who’s been serving the islands between Parry Sound and Moon River since 2016. I’ve been a cottager in the area my entire life and am prepared, equipped, and ready to handle any project on the Bay.

My season generally lasts from early-June until the end of September.

I offer, for every service, a firm, quoted price. I believe this is the fairest structure for both parties. The customer gets the security of knowing the cost before committing (and thus the ability to haggle or shop elsewhere), and I charge the amount that I think the job is worth. Personally, I find charging by the hour to be undignified.

item.title: Exterior Painting
item.description:
I offer modern, efficient, large-scale exterior painting. Modern; I use the most modern equipment and tools as the basis of my process. Efficient; my process is effective and practiced. Large-scale; my process can handle any size job on the Bay.

Process

  1. Estimation
  2. Washing
  3. Preparation
  4. Painting
  5. Cleanup

Estimation

I derive my price from two numbers:

  1. The estimated number of days it will take to complete the job
  2. The estimated number of gallons of paint required

I generate estimates with the Invoice Manager app and send them to the client either via email or text.

item.title: Insurance
item.description:
Cottage Painter is fully insured by Lloyd’s through Martin Merry & Reid Limited. I have redacted my mailing address in the public copy provided here for privacy reasons. Contact me if you require the unredacted, official copy.

Download 🡄 🡆 Page: /

item.title: Terms and Conditions
item.description:
Table of Contents

  • Key Words
  • Contractor Obligations
  • Client Jobsite Obligations

Key Words

  • Contractor: Currie Carmichael, operating as Cottage Painter
  • Daily Rate: Per-day cost of labour stated on the Estimate
  • Hidden Condition: A problem not known in advance, such as rot, rust, moisture, mold, failed old paint, hazardous materials, or structural issues.
  • Job: The work to be done by the Contractor described in the Estimate.
  • Jobsite: Where the Job is done.
  • Timeframe: The time scheduled for the Job.

Contractor Obligations

  1. Maintain a High-Standard of Craftsmanship: Do the Job in a good and workmanlike manner, consistent with generally accepted trade practices.
  2. Supply Material: Supply the labour, tools, and materials, unless the Estimate says otherwise.
  3. Be Reliable: Finish within the Timeframe. Delays due to weather, manufacturer delays, Hidden Conditions, or Client-imposed delays are not the Contractor’s fault.
  4. Be Orderly: Keep the Jobsite reasonably tidy for the duration of the Timeframe and leave it clean upon completion.
  5. Be Insured: Carry Commercial General Liability insurance (see Proof of Insurance) and maintain WSIB coverage as required by Ontario law.

Client Obligations

The Client must provide the following to the Contractor:

item.title: Hiring
item.description:
Looking for a summer job? Look no further! Join the greatest band of painters ever assembled on Georgian Bay for an epic, summer-long adventure of:

  • Backbrushing…
    • Brush arm and leg through hundreds, potentially thousands of gallons of paint as you struggle to push viscous liquid deep within the cavernous pores of wood
  • Caulking…
    • Caulk where no man has caulked before; Grab that Caulk and Caulk baby; CAAAAAULKKKKK
  • Masking…
    • Defend thousands of square-feet of window and deck from the dreaded overspray; block its vain attempts to destroy my reputation
  • Moving…
    • Pickup things in one location and move them to another using the magic of the human motor system
  • Painting…
    • …painting…?…what the hell is paintin…oh yah i remember - honestly you probably won’t do too much painting.

You are Qualified if:

  • you are at least 16 years old
  • you are available between June and September
  • you have access to a boat
  • you have no experience painting
  • you have lots of experience painting
  • you found the previous section at least slightly amusing

You are REALLY Qualified if:

  • you are available from the beginning of June until the end of September, potentially Thanksgiving

Payment

I pay newbies $150 a day plus gas. That number will increase proportionally to the amount of responsibility you are willing and able to take on.
